rag_rules: If AGREE reports type mismatches or unresolved identifiers, trace the root cause to invalid helper declarations or references on the same line and fix or remove the clause.
When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and avoid introducing package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex.
Type consistency is required for all AGREE expressions; mismatched types indicate invalid helper or assumption declarations.
Repair actions should target specific diagnostics without altering unrelated architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Helper declarations like `eq` and `const` must specify correct scalar types: int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types that are not declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not add new architecture elements when fixing AGREE diagnostics; only modify existing clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component port usage in type contracts
